errors.title: attempt to index nil with '…'
errors.description: Standard Luau runtime error in Roblox Studio: you used . or : on nil (missing instance, FindFirstChild returned nil, or value not ready yet).
fixes: Guard with if x then … end before indexing
Use WaitForChild("Name") (and a timeout) instead of assuming the child exists
Check spelling and that the object is parented before you read it

errors.title: FindFirstChild returned nil
errors.description: Stack or context mentions FindFirstChild—often the next line is attempt to index nil because the result was not checked.
fixes: Check if result exists before using it: local c = parent:FindFirstChild("X"); if c then … end
Use WaitForChild("X") when the object must exist (with a timeout if needed)

errors.title: table index is nil
errors.description: Luau error when nil is used as a table key in an operation that does not allow it.
fixes: Ensure the key variable is set before t[key] = … or t[key]
Use a sentinel or default key only if your logic allows it

errors.title: attempt to perform arithmetic on nil
errors.description: Luau error when +, -, *, /, etc. involve nil (often an uninitialized stat or missing Value object).
fixes: Default with x = x or 0 only when that makes sense
Ensure IntValue/NumberValue exists before reading .Value
Validate with typeof(x) == "number" before math

errors.title: attempt to concatenate nil with string
errors.description: Luau error when building a string with .. and one side is nil.
fixes: Use tostring(x) or (x or "") when building messages
Check the variable is set before concatenating

errors.title: attempt to get length of a nil value
errors.description: Luau error when # is used on nil (table or string was never assigned).
fixes: Ensure the table exists before #t
Use if t then local n = #t end

errors.title: … is not a valid member of …
errors.description: Roblox DataModel error: wrong property/child name, wrong class, or the instance is not parented where you think.
fixes: Fix spelling and capitalization (names are case-sensitive)
Use FindFirstChild / WaitForChild for children; use correct API for properties
Confirm you're indexing the right Instance type (check ClassName)

errors.title: Infinite yield possible on '…'
errors.description: Roblox Studio warning: WaitForChild (or similar) waited past the threshold because the child never appeared.
fixes: Ensure the instance is created under the parent you expect
Pass a timeout: WaitForChild("X", timeoutSeconds)
Fix typos in child names and replication order (server creates before client waits)

errors.title: WaitForChild timeout / long wait
errors.description: The line mentions WaitForChild—often a hang, slow replication, or missing child (Studio may also show Infinite yield possible for the same issue).
fixes: Use WaitForChild("X", timeLimitSeconds) so the call fails fast instead of hanging
Ensure the server (or owning side) creates and parents the instance before the client waits
Confirm the child name spelling and that it is not destroyed before the wait completes

errors.title: The Parent property of … is nil, current parent: …
errors.description: Appears when parenting or hierarchy logic assumes a Parent that is not set (or object was unparented).
fixes: Parent the instance to Workspace or a folder before relying on the tree
Check obj.Parent before walking up the tree

errors.title: bad argument #… to '…' (…)
errors.description: Luau error: wrong type, order, or count of arguments to a built-in or engine function.
fixes: Read the Roblox API page for that function's signature
Print typeof() for each argument
Avoid passing nil where an Instance, string, or number is required

errors.title: invalid argument #… to '…'
errors.description: Luau error: argument rejected (often type or range) for a C/API function.
fixes: Match the documented parameter types
Validate numbers are finite and in range before calling

errors.title: missing argument #… to '…'
errors.description: Luau error: a required parameter was omitted.
fixes: Pass all required arguments in order
Check for optional overloads in the docs

errors.title: Script timeout: exhausted allowed execution time
errors.description: Roblox: your script ran too long in one go (tight loop or heavy work without yielding).
fixes: Add task.wait() or break work across Heartbeat/Stepped
Avoid infinite while true with no yield
Profile and move heavy work out of hot paths

errors.title: Execution of script exhausted allowed execution time
errors.description: Roblox: same class of issue as script timeout—too much synchronous work.
fixes: Yield periodically in loops
Split work with task.defer / task.spawn where appropriate

errors.title: FireServer can only be called from the client
errors.messagePattern: fireserver can only be called from the client
errors.description: Roblox networking: FireServer was called from a server Script; only LocalScripts (client) may call FireServer.
fixes: Move FireServer into a LocalScript under PlayerGui / StarterPlayer / character
On the server, listen with OnServerEvent instead of firing from server to fake client calls

errors.title: FireClient can only be called from the server
errors.messagePattern: fireclient can only be called from the server
errors.description: Roblox: FireClient/FireAllClients must run in a server Script, not a LocalScript.
fixes: Call FireClient from ServerScriptService or another server script
Have the client ask the server via FireServer, then server fires back

errors.title: FireAllClients can only be called from the server
errors.messagePattern: fireallclients can only be called from the server
errors.description: Roblox: same rule as FireClient—all-client remotes are server-only.
fixes: Run FireAllClients from a Script, not a LocalScript

errors.title: InvokeServer can only be called from the client
errors.messagePattern: invokeserver can only be called from the client
errors.description: Roblox: RemoteFunction:InvokeServer is client-only; the server uses OnServerInvoke.
fixes: Call InvokeServer from a LocalScript
On server, assign function to OnServerInvoke and return values to the client

errors.title: divide by zero
errors.description: Luau arithmetic error when denominator is 0.
fixes: Check if d ~= 0 before dividing
Clamp or skip the operation when invalid

errors.title: unexpected symbol near '…'
errors.description: Luau parse error: the parser hit a token it did not expect at that position.
fixes: Click the error line in the script editor and match if/then/end, function/end, do/end
Check quotes and commas in tables
Fix typos near the column the parser points to

errors.title: Expected … (parse error)
errors.description: Luau parse error mentioning expected tokens, e.g. ')' expected (to close '(' at line X) or Expected identifier when parsing expression. The word unexpected contains expected as a substring—use higher priority than this row for unexpected-symbol lines (see LUA_SYNTAX_UNEXPECTED).
fixes: Close open brackets, strings, or blocks
Add missing then, end, do, or comma in tables
Fix typos where the parser points

errors.title: attempt to call a nil value
errors.messagePattern: attempt to call a nil value
errors.description: Luau error: you used () on something that is nil (missing module export, unset callback, or wrong variable).
fixes: Ensure the function was assigned before calling
Check require() actually returned your module table with that method
Search for typos in the function name

errors.title: attempt to call a … value
errors.description: Fallback Luau error: call () on a non-function type (number, table, Instance, boolean, etc.). Pattern has no trailing space; prefer LUA_CALL_NIL when the line is attempt to call a nil value.
fixes: Confirm you are not overwriting a function with data
Use typeof(f) == "function" before calling unknown values

errors.title: attempt to compare nil with number / incompatible types
errors.description: Luau error: ordering or comparison with nil or incompatible types.
fixes: Compare to nil explicitly: if x == nil then …
Convert types before comparing (<, >) when needed

errors.title: Module code did not return exactly one value
errors.messagePattern: module code did not return exactly one value
errors.description: Roblox ModuleScript error: the file must return exactly one value (usually one table).
fixes: End the module with return M (single table/object)
Remove code paths that return nothing or return multiple values
Avoid bare return with no value on the main path

errors.title: Requested module experienced an error while loading
errors.description: Roblox: require() failed because the required ModuleScript errored during load.
fixes: Open the module path from the stack trace and fix the inner error first
Use pcall(require) if you need to handle failure

errors.title: circular dependency detected while calling require()
errors.description: Roblox: two or more ModuleScripts require each other in a cycle.
fixes: Move shared code into a third module both can require
Lazy-require inside a function after load completes
Restructure so modules only depend one way

errors.title: stack overflow
errors.description: Luau: call depth exceeded—usually infinite or very deep recursion.
fixes: Add a base case to recursion
Rewrite as a loop
Check accidental self-calls

errors.title: … has been destroyed
errors.description: Roblox: code used an Instance after Destroy() or after it left the game.
fixes: Disconnect connections and clear references on cleanup
Check obj.Parent or obj:IsDescendantOf(game) before use
Avoid storing Instances long after gameplay moves on

errors.title: HttpService is not enabled
errors.description: Roblox: Game Settings disallow HttpService requests.
fixes: Studio: Game Settings > Security > enable Allow HTTP requests
Published game: enable the same in the place’s settings on the website

errors.title: HttpError: … (HttpService request failed)
errors.description: Roblox HttpService: request failed (status, TLS, timeout, or policy). The suffix names the failure kind.
fixes: Check URL, Method, and Headers
Whitelist domains if your game uses that feature
Retry on transient errors with backoff

errors.title: HttpError: ConnectFail
errors.description: Roblox could not open a TCP/TLS connection to the host.
fixes: Verify hostname and https://
Confirm the API is online
Check firewall / DNS issues outside Roblox if testing endpoints yourself

errors.title: 502: API Services rejected request with error. HttpError: …
errors.description: Roblox backend rejected the request (overload, maintenance, or invalid request). Often seen with DataStore or cloud APIs.
fixes: Retry with exponential backoff
Reduce request rate and payload size
Confirm experience has API access (published place, settings)

errors.title: Request was throttled
errors.description: Roblox rate-limited the operation (DataStore or similar high-volume usage).
fixes: Spread reads/writes over time
Cache on the server
Use UpdateAsync and avoid redundant SetAsync in loops

errors.title: Unable to cast … to …
errors.description: Luau typed mode / engine conversion: value could not convert to the expected type.
fixes: Validate or construct the correct type before passing in
For Instances, check ClassName:IsA("ExpectedClass")

errors.title: LocalPlayer is not a valid member of Players "Players"
errors.messagePattern: localplayer is not a valid member of players
errors.description: Roblox: Players.LocalPlayer is nil on the server; only the client has a local player.
fixes: Use the Player from PlayerAdded / event parameters on the server
Put LocalPlayer-only code in a LocalScript
Use remotes to ask the server with explicit player argument

errors.title: … can only be used on the client
errors.messagePattern: can only be used on the client
errors.description: Roblox security/context: this API is restricted to the client session.
fixes: Move the call to a LocalScript under an allowed container
Mirror state to the server with remotes if needed

errors.title: … can only be used on the server
errors.messagePattern: can only be used on the server
errors.description: Roblox security/context: this API must run in a server Script.
fixes: Move logic to ServerScriptService (or another server script)
Have the client request the action via RemoteEvent/RemoteFunction

errors.title: attempt to yield across metamethod/C-call boundary
errors.description: Classic Luau error when task.wait/yield runs in a context that cannot yield (some metamethods and engine call paths).
fixes: Defer with task.defer or RunService events instead of waiting inside __index/__newindex
Restructure so waits happen in normal thread execution
